transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Hello, this is Agent Williams from the IRS. We have detected a serious issue with your tax return from last year.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: Oh my, what kind of issue?

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: You owe $4,500 in back taxes. If you do not pay immediately, a warrant will be issued for your arrest today.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: Arrest? That sounds very serious...

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Yes ma'am. But we can resolve this right now if you purchase gift cards worth $4,500 and read me the codes. This is the only way to avoid arrest.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: Gift cards? That seems unusual.

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: This is standard government procedure. You must act NOW or officers will be dispatched to your home within the hour.

description: Classic IRS impersonation scam with arrest threats and gift card payment demand.

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Good afternoon. This is the fraud department at Bank of America. We have detected unauthorized activity on your account.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: Oh no, what happened?

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Someone has been making large purchases on your debit card. We need to verify your identity to secure your account.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: Okay, what do you need?

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: I'll need your full Social Security number and your online banking password to verify your identity.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: My Social Security number? Let me think...

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Ma'am, every second counts. If we don't act now, they could drain your entire account. Please provide your SSN immediately.

description: Bank impersonation scam attempting to harvest Social Security number and banking credentials.

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Hello, this is Microsoft Technical Support. We have detected a virus on your computer that is sending your personal data to hackers.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: A virus? I don't know much about computers.

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Don't worry, I can help you fix this right now. I just need you to go to your computer and download a remote access tool so I can clean the virus.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: Okay, what do I need to do?

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Go to this website and download the software. It will let me connect to your computer remotely. Also, the repair service costs $299, payable by wire transfer.

description: Tech support scam requesting remote access and wire transfer payment.

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Hi, this is Sarah calling from Dr. Smith's office. We're confirming your appointment for next Tuesday at 2 PM.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: Oh yes, thank you for the reminder.

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Great! Just a reminder to bring your insurance card. See you Tuesday!

description: Legitimate call — doctor's office appointment reminder. Should NOT trigger alerts.

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Grandma? It's me, Tommy. I'm in trouble and I need your help.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: Tommy? You don't sound like yourself...

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: I got into a car accident and I'm at the hospital. I need $3,000 for bail money. Please don't tell Mom and Dad.

transcript_chunks.speaker: elder
transcript_chunks.text: Oh my goodness! Are you okay?

transcript_chunks.speaker: caller
transcript_chunks.text: Please wire the money right away. My lawyer says I need it within the next hour or I'll have to stay in jail overnight.

description: Classic grandparent scam — impersonating a grandchild in distress requesting emergency wire transfer.
